Name: Palomar Knot
Description: The Palomar knot is one of the strongest and simplest knots to tie. It retains 95% of the original line strength and is ideal for lures and hooks. Perfect for beginners.
Required Material: Braided or monofilament line Fluorocarbon recommended
Tips: Double the line before passing through the eyelet.

Name: Grinner Knot (Uni)
Description: The Grinner or Uni knot is an extremely versatile universal knot. It works with all types of lines and can be used for hooks lures and swivels. 90% strength.
Tips: Wet the knot before tightening to avoid overheating.

Name: Improved Clinch Knot
Description: Improved version of the classic Clinch with an extra pass that increases strength to 95%. Excellent for eyelet hooks and lures.
Tips: Wrap the line 5-6 times around the main body.

Name: Hook Loop Knot
Description: Creates a loop at the base of the hook for more natural movement of the lure or bait. Ideal for soft lures.
Tips: The loop gives more freedom to the lure.

Name: Rapala Knot
Description: The Rapala knot creates a non-slipping loop perfect for hard lures. It allows the lure to swim naturally with maximum amplitude.

Name: Blood Knot
Description: Classic knot for connecting two lines of similar diameters. Often used to add a fluorocarbon leader. 80% strength.
Tips: Alternate wraps on each side for balance.

Name: Albright Knot
Description: The Albright knot is perfect for connecting braid to fluoro or nylon. It passes easily through guides and is very compact. 90% strength.
Tips: Tighten gradually by pulling on both strands.

Name: FG Knot
Description: Modern knot considered the best braid-to-fluoro connection. Very thin it passes perfectly through guides. Strength close to 100%.

Name: Double Surgeon Knot
Description: Simple and quick knot for connecting two lines even of different diameters. Less thin than Albright but easier to tie.
Tips: Pass both lines together through the loops.

Name: Dropper Knot
Description: Essential knot for creating side leaders on a rig. Used in surfcasting and paternoster to present multiple baits.
Tips: Leave enough length for the leader.

Name: Paternoster Loop Knot
Description: Creates a fixed loop on the main line to attach a leader. Traditionally used in England for surfcasting.

Name: Triple Loop Knot
Description: Allows creating three droppers on the same main line. Ideal for presenting multiple baits at different heights.

Name: Thunder Knot
Description: Robust and easy knot for fixing hooks and lures on thick diameter. Excellent for conger and meagre fishing.

Name: Snell Knot
Description: The Snell knot is specially designed for hooks without eyelet (spade end). It wraps the line around the shank for maximum solidity.

Name: Perfection Loop Knot
Description: Creates a perfect loop perfectly round and non-slipping. Used for leaders and lure connections.

Name: Kreh Knot (Non-slip loop)
Description: Slipping loop knot that never comes undone. Ideal for soft lures and hooks. 95% strength.

Name: Barrel Knot
Description: Ancient knot for connecting two lines. Less used today but remains effective for nylon lines of the same diameter.

Name: 100% Knot
Description: Modern knot designed to preserve 100% of line strength. Thin and compact it passes perfectly through guides.
